questions.question: In which hemisphere is India located?
questions.correct_answer: India lies entirely in the Northern Hemisphere.
questions.explanation: The mainland of India extends between latitudes 8°4'N and 37°6'N, which are all north of the Equator.

questions.question: What is the latitudinal extent of India?
questions.explanation: These latitudes mark the southernmost and northernmost points of the Indian mainland.

questions.question: What is the longitudinal extent of India?
questions.explanation: These longitudes mark the westernmost and easternmost boundaries of India.

questions.question: Which important latitude divides India into two parts?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer divides India into tropical and subtropical regions.

questions.question: What is the total geographical area of India?
questions.explanation: India's large area makes it the seventh largest country in the world.

questions.question: Which longitude is used as the Standard Meridian of India?
questions.explanation: This longitude passing near Mirzapur in Uttar Pradesh is used to calculate Indian Standard Time.

questions.question: Which seas surround India on the west and east?
questions.correct_answer: Arabian Sea in the west and Bay of Bengal in the east
questions.explanation: These two seas form the coastal boundaries of the Indian peninsula.

questions.question: Why is India's location important for trade?
questions.correct_answer: Because it lies centrally between East and West Asia along major trade routes.
questions.explanation: India's location helped ancient trade and cultural exchanges with many civilizations.

questions.question: Name the island groups of India.
questions.correct_answer: Lakshadweep Islands and Andaman & Nicobar Islands
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep lies in the Arabian Sea while Andaman & Nicobar lie in the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Which island countries are India's southern neighbours?
questions.explanation: Sri Lanka is separated by the Palk Strait and Maldives lies southwest of Lakshadweep.

notes.title: Location of India
notes.content: India lies in the Northern Hemisphere and is located in South Asia. Its mainland extends between 8°4'N and 37°6'N latitudes and 68°7'E and 97°25'E longitudes.

notes.title: Size of India
notes.content: India covers an area of about 3.28 million square kilometres and is the seventh largest country in the world.

notes.title: Indian Standard Time
notes.content: The longitude 82°30'E is used as the Standard Meridian of India to maintain uniform time across the country.

notes.title: India's Central Location
notes.content: India's central location in Asia helped establish trade and cultural links with Europe, Africa and East Asia.

notes.title: Neighbouring Countries
notes.content: India shares land boundaries with Pakistan, Afghanistan, China, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Myanmar.

facts.fact: India accounts for about 2.4% of the total geographical area of the world.
India has a land boundary of about 15,200 km.
The total coastline of India including islands is about 7,516.6 km.
The Indian Ocean is named after India due to its prominent position.
